title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)
label: live_repo_v2_transmute_candidate
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B5-combinatorial-probability-random-chords
Title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)

Question:
Disk divided into 4 quadrants.
Add 25 random chords with endpoints in different quadrants.
What is the expected number of regions?

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: 326
JUSTIFICATION: The maximum number of regions formed by n chords inside a circle, with no three chords intersecting at a single interior point and no chords sharing endpoints, is given by R = 1 + n + C(n,2), where C(n,2) = n(n-1)/2 is the number of intersection points. Here, n = 25 chords connecting different quadrants, so no chords share endpoints. Therefore, maximum intersection
